# WordPress 6.x — Tech Pack

## 1. Fingerprinting Signals

| Signal | Type | Value | Confidence |
|--------|------|-------|------------|
| `wp-content/` in HTML | HTML substring | `wp-content/` | High |
| `wp-json/` in HTML links | HTML substring | `wp-json/` | High |
| `wp-includes/` in HTML | HTML substring | `wp-includes/` | High |
| Generator meta tag | HTML meta | `WordPress {version}` | Version |
| `wordpress_logged_in_*` | Cookie | prefix match | High |
| Wappalyzer result | MCP | `"WordPress"` | Definitive |

**Version extraction:**
```bash
curl -s {site} | grep -oP 'content="WordPress \K[\d.]+'
# Also try:
curl -s {site}/wp-json/ | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('name',''))"
```

## 2. Default API Surfaces

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `/wp-json/` | GET | None | REST API root — lists all namespaces |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/` | GET | None | Core namespace — lists all routes |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/posts` | GET | None | Published posts (default: 10) |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/pages` | GET | None | Published pages |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/categories` | GET | None | All categories |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/tags` | GET | None | All tags |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/media` | GET | None | Public media library |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/users` | GET | None* | *Email hidden; may be blocked by plugin |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/search` | GET | None | Search posts/pages by keyword |
| `/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` | POST | Varies | Legacy AJAX (always accessible) |
| `/wp-sitemap.xml` | GET | None | Built-in sitemap (WP 5.5+) |
| `/feed/` | GET | None | RSS feed |
| `/xmlrpc.php` | POST | Varies | Legacy API (often active) |

## 3. Config / Constants Locations

| Location | What's there | How to access |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `wp-config.php` | DB creds, secret keys, table prefix | Source only |
| `/wp-json/` root response | REST namespace list, site name, URL | Public GET |
| `window.wpApiSettings` | REST nonce, root URL | JS eval in browser |
| `window.wp.apiFetch` | REST fetch wrapper | JS eval |
| `data-nonce` HTML attributes | Per-form nonces | HTML scrape |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/settings` | Site title, tagline, URL | Admin auth required |

## 4. Auth Patterns

| Pattern | Location | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| WP REST nonce (`X-WP-Nonce` header) | `wp_create_nonce('wp_rest')` injected in page | Valid ~24h; fetch from `window.wpApiSettings.nonce` |
| Application passwords | Basic auth header | WP 5.6+; `user:app-password` base64 encoded |
| Cookie auth | `wordpress_logged_in_*` | Session cookie; browser-only |
| JWT (plugin-dependent) | Bearer token | Requires JWT plugin |

**Nonce acquisition (authenticated operations):**
The REST nonce is injected into `window.wpApiSettings.nonce` when WordPress loads
`wp-api.min.js`. Also available at `window.wp.apiFetch.nonceMiddleware.nonce`.
Fetch via browser JS eval or grep inline scripts for `"nonce":"`.

## 5. JS Bundle Patterns

| Path | Content |
|------|---------|
| `/wp-content/themes/{theme}/assets/js/*.js` | Theme JS (may have source maps) |
| `/wp-content/plugins/{plugin}/assets/*.js` | Plugin JS |
| `/wp-includes/js/wp-api-*.min.js` | WP REST API client |
| `/wp-includes/js/wp-util.min.js` | AJAX utilities |

Check each bundle URL + `.map` suffix for source maps.

## 6. Source Map Patterns

WordPress core JS files do NOT have source maps in production.
Custom themes compiled with webpack/vite/rollup MAY have source maps.
Check: `{theme-bundle}.js.map` — if 200, recover original source paths.

## 7. Common Plugins & Extensions

| Plugin | API it adds | Detection signal |
|--------|------------|-----------------|
| WooCommerce | `/wp-json/wc/v3/` (products, orders, customers) | `woocommerce` cookie or HTML |
| ACF Pro | `/wp-json/acf/v3/` | `acf/` in source, admin menu |
| WPML | `/wp-json/wpml/` | `wpml_*` cookies |
| Gravity Forms | `/wp-json/gf/v2/` | `gravityforms` in HTML |
| Contact Form 7 | `/wp-json/contact-form-7/v1/` | `cf7` in HTML |
| Yoast SEO | `/wp-json/yoast/v1/` | `yoast` in HTML |
| JWT Auth | `/wp-json/jwt-auth/v1/token` | Response to JWT endpoints |
| WooCommerce REST API | `/wp-json/wc/v1/`, `/wp-json/wc/v2/` | Legacy WC versions |

## 8. Known Public Data

| Endpoint | Data | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/categories?per_page=100` | All categories with IDs | Useful for constants.md |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/tags?per_page=100` | All tags with IDs | — |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/posts?per_page=1` | Latest post (schema sample) | Reveals custom fields |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/types` | All registered post types | Reveals custom post types |
| `/wp-json/wp/v2/taxonomies` | All taxonomies | Reveals custom taxonomies |
| `/feed/?cat={id}` | Category-filtered RSS | Use category IDs from /v2/categories |
| `/wp-sitemap.xml` | Sitemap index | Links to per-type sitemaps |

## 9. Probe Checklist

Run these in order. Record result (✓ 200 / ✗ 403 / – 404) for each.

- [ ] `GET /wp-json/` — REST API root (lists namespaces; confirms REST is enabled)
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/` — Core namespace (lists all routes with methods)
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/posts?per_page=1` — Confirm public posts accessible
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/categories?per_page=100` — Full category list with IDs
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/tags?per_page=100` — Full tag list with IDs
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/users?per_page=10` — User list (may be 403)
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/types` — Custom post types
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/taxonomies` — Custom taxonomies
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/search?search=test` — Search endpoint
- [ ] `GET /wp-sitemap.xml` — Sitemap (WP 5.5+)
- [ ] `GET /feed/` — RSS feed
- [ ] `POST /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` with `action=heartbeat` — AJAX endpoint
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/v3/` — WooCommerce (if WC detected)
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/acf/v3/` — ACF (if detected)
- [ ] Check `/wp-json/` namespace list for any non-core namespaces
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/` — enumerate ALL namespaces (not just core), check for custom post types and taxonomies
- [ ] `POST /xmlrpc.php` with `system.listMethods` — enumerate XML-RPC methods available
- [ ] Check for WordPress multisite: `GET /sites/` or look for `blog_id` in cookies/source
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/settings` — attempt to access site settings (requires auth, confirms auth setup)
- [ ] Check for REST API namespace plugins: grep `/wp-json/` response for non-core namespaces like `acf/v3`, `woocommerce`, `yoast/v1`
- [ ] `POST /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` with `action=query_themes` — test AJAX endpoint with various actions
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wp/v2/users?per_page=100` — attempt user enumeration (may be blocked by security plugins)

## 10. Gotchas

- **REST API can be completely disabled.** Plugins like "Disable REST API" return 401/403
  on all `/wp-json/` requests. If this happens: fall back to AJAX endpoint + XML-RPC.
- **XML-RPC is often active** even on secured sites. Try `POST /xmlrpc.php` with
  `<methodCall><methodName>system.listMethods</methodName></methodCall>`.
- **Nonce stability.** The REST nonce changes daily (based on WP secret key + salt),
  NOT per-request. Fetch once at session start and reuse.
- **User enumeration blocking.** Many security plugins block `/wp-json/wp/v2/users`.
  A 403 here is expected; note it but don't treat as a site-wide auth issue.
- **Namespace sprawl.** Large sites have 20+ namespaces. Always read the full namespace
  list from `/wp-json/` and probe each non-standard one.
- **WooCommerce API versioning.** WC uses `/wc/v1/`, `/wc/v2/`, `/wc/v3/`. Always test
  the latest (`v3`) first; older versions may still be active.
- **REST API authentication changes in WP 6.x.** Application Passwords (introduced 5.6)
  are the preferred auth method for REST API. Cookie+nonce still works for browser-based calls.

## 11. GitHub Code Search Patterns

Use these queries on GitHub to find custom endpoints, plugin code, and configuration examples for this framework.

### Framework-Specific Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `"register_rest_route" language:php` | Custom REST API endpoints |
| `"add_action('rest_api_init" language:php` | REST API initialization hooks |
| `"wp_ajax_" language:php` | Legacy AJAX action handlers |
| `"register_post_type" language:php` | Custom post type definitions |

### Example Queries for WordPress

```bash
# Search for custom API routes/endpoints
site:github.com "WordPress" "api" filetype:php "rest_route"

# Search for authentication patterns
site:github.com "WordPress" "auth" "rest_api_init" language:php

# Search for configuration files with endpoint definitions
site:github.com "WordPress" "register_rest_route" "endpoint" language:php

# Search for custom post types, taxonomies, or extensions
site:github.com "WordPress" "register_post_type" "custom" language:php
```

## 12. Framework-Specific Google Dorks

Use these Google search queries to discover exposed endpoints, configuration files, and documentation for this framework.

### Discovery Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `site:{domain} inurl:/wp-json/` | WordPress REST API endpoints and namespaces |
| `site:{domain} inurl:/wp-admin/` | WordPress admin panel and login paths |
| `site:{domain} inurl:/wp-content/themes/` | WordPress theme files and template parts |
| `site:{domain} "wp-content/uploads/"` | Exposed uploaded media and assets |

### Complete Dork List for WordPress

```
# API endpoints
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-json/
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-json/wp/v2/
site:{domain} inurl:/xmlrpc.php

# Framework-specific paths
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-admin/
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-content/themes/
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-content/plugins/

# Configuration files
site:{domain} filetype:php wp-config
site:{domain} filetype:json "wp-json"

# Documentation/leaks
site:{domain} "WordPress" "api" "endpoint"
site:{domain} "wordpress_logged_in" "cookie"

# Admin/debug paths
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-admin/install.php
```

## 13. Cross-Cutting OSINT Patterns

These patterns apply across frameworks and should be checked for any detected technology.

### Favicon Hashing

Identify technology stack by hashing favicon and searching Shodan/Censys for same stack:

```bash
# Get favicon hash (mmh3 hash of favicon content)
curl -s "https://{domain}/favicon.ico" | python3 -c "
import sys, hashlib, base64
data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
# Simple mmh3 hash simulation using Python
import mmh3 2>/dev/null || pip install mmh3
# Or use: python3 -c "import mmh3; print(mmh3.hash(data))"
print('Favicon hash needed for Shodan search: icon_hash')
"

# Search Shodan for same favicon (indicates shadow IT subdomains)
# site:shodan.io search: icon_hash:{hash}
```

**What it reveals:** Hidden subdomains running same framework stack as main site.

### Source Map Discovery

Check for source maps across all JS bundles:

```bash
# Extract all JS bundle URLs from HTML
curl -s "https://{domain}/" | grep -oP 'src="[^"]+\.js[^"]*"' | grep -oP '"[^"]+' | tr -d '"' > js_urls.txt

# Check each for .map file
while read url; do
  map_url="${url}.map"
  status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${map_url}")
  [ "$status" = "200" ] && echo "SOURCE MAP: ${map_url}"
done < js_urls.txt
```

**Build tool patterns:**
| Build Tool | Source Map Pattern | Detection |
|------------|-------------------|------------|
| Webpack | `{bundle}.js.map` or `//# sourceMappingURL=` | Check response header `X-SourceMap` |
| Vite | `{name}-[hash].js.map` | Vite manifest `manifest.json` |
| Rollup | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| esbuild | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| Next.js | `/_next/static/chunks/*.js.map` | Only if `productionBrowserSourceMaps: true` |

### Tech Stack → API Pattern Mapping

Auto-map detected frameworks to likely endpoint patterns:

| Framework | Common API Patterns |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Next.js | `/api/*`, `/_next/data/*`, `/api/auth/*`, `/api/trpc/*` |
| WordPress | `/wp-json/*`, `/wp-json/wp/v2/*`, `/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` |
| Shopify | `/api/2024-10/graphql.json`, `/products.json`, `/collections.json` |
| Rails | `/api/v1/*`, `/assets/*`, `/users/sign_in` |
| Laravel | `/api/*`, `/livewire/message/*`, `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` |
| Strapi | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/api/upload*` |
| Magento | `/rest/V1/*`, `/pub/static/*` |
| Django | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/accounts/*` |

When Phase 3 detects a framework, use this table to prioritize Phase 5/6/7 probes.

### Email Naming Convention Analysis

Extract emails from theHarvester/GitHub results to predict internal subdomains:

```bash
# Sample emails found: john.doe@example.com, jane.smith@example.com
# Predicted subdomains: mail.example.com, smtp.example.com, exchange.example.com

# Common patterns:
# first.last@ → internal.example.com, mail.example.com
# firstinitial+last@ → owa.example.com, outlook.example.com
```

**Add to Phase 9 session brief:** Note email patterns and predicted subdomains.
